accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and accurate context about the BCOR program/PHSA Section 547 is verified.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the summary sticks closely to the bill text without invented details.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose as reauthorizing and increasing funding for the building communities of recovery grants.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly states the bill amends Section 547(f) of the Public Health Service Act and describes the strike-and-replace.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill is a simple funding reauthorization with no material exceptions or conditions to note.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the authorization applies to fiscal years 2026 through 2030.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the bill's sole operative effect: changing the authorization amount and fiscal years.

objective_tone.why: Neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

concise.why: Somewhat repetitive of the FY2026-2030 figures but generally tight and free of major filler.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the Public Health Service Act, Section 547(f), and the bill's short title.

correct_figures.why: All figures ($5M FY2019-2023, $17M FY2026-2030) match the bill text exactly.
